title: Calibrating elevator actuator for disk drive
abstract: A data storage device is disclosed comprising a first disk comprising a first disk surface, a second disk comprising a second disk surface, an elevator actuator configured to actuate a head along an axial dimension relative to the first and second disks, a radial actuator configured to actuate the head radially over the first disk surface or the second disk surface, and a position sensor configured to generate a sinusoidal sensor signal representing a position of the head along the axial dimension. A crash stop_offset along the axial dimension is measured from a crash stop position of the elevator actuator to a zero crossing of the sinusoidal sensor signal.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A data storage device comprising: a first disk comprising a first disk surface; a second disk comprising a second disk surface; a head; an elevator actuator configured to actuate the head along an axial dimension relative to the first and second disks; a radial actuator configured to actuate the head radially over the first disk surface or the second disk surface; a position sensor configured to generate a sinusoidal sensor signal representing a position of the head along the axial dimension; and control circuitry configured to measure a crashstop offset along the axial dimension from a crashstop position of the elevator actuator to a zero crossing of the sinusoidal sensor signal 2. The data storage device as recited in claim 1 , wherein the zero crossing represents a home position of the head along the axial dimension. 3. The data storage device as recited in claim 2, wherein the control circuitry is further configured to: control the elevator actuator to move the head to the home position; and after moving the head to the home position, control the elevator actuator to move the head along the axial dimension a distance: D1 -crashstop_offset where D1 represents a distance along the axial dimension from the crashstop position to the first disk surface and crashstop_offset represents the crashstop offset. 4. The data storage device as recited in claim 3, wherein the control circuitry is further configured to move the head to the home position when the data storage device is powered on. 5. The data storage device as recited in claim 1 , wherein the control circuitry is further configured to measure the crashstop offset by: controlling the elevator actuator to move the head in a first direction along the axial dimension until the crashstop position is detected; and after detecting the crashstop position, controlling the elevator actuator to move the head in a second direction opposite the first direction along the axial dimension until the zero crossing is detected. 6. The data storage device as recited in claim 1 , wherein the control circuitry is further configured to measure the crashstop offset by: controlling the elevator actuator to move the head between the crashstop position and the zero crossing multiple times to generate a plurality of measured crashstop offsets; and estimate the crashstop_offset based on the plurality of measured crashstop offsets. 7. The data storage device as recited in claim 1 , further comprising an actuator arm, wherein: the head is coupled to a distal end of the actuator arm; and the elevator actuator actuates the actuator arm along the axial dimension. 8. The data storage device as recited in claim 1 , further comprising an actuator arm and a ramp for loading/unloading the head, wherein: the head is coupled to a distal end of the actuator arm; and the elevator actuator actuates the ramp along the axial dimension 9. A data storage device comprising: a first disk comprising a first disk surface; a second disk comprising a second disk surface; an actuator arm; a head coupled to a distal end of the actuator arm; a ramp for loading/unloading the head; a first elevator actuator configured to actuate the actuator arm along an axial dimension relative to the first and second disks; a first position sensor configured to generate a first sensor signal representing a displacement of the actuator arm along the axial dimension; a second elevator actuator configured to actuate at least part of the ramp along the axial dimension; a second position sensor configured to generate a second sensor signal representing a displacement of the head along the axial dimension; and a radial actuator configured to actuate the head radially over the first disk surface or the second disk surface. 10. The data storage device as recited in claim 9, further comprising control circuitry configured to move the first elevator actuator to a first crashstop position along the axial dimension based on a first default velocity command and simultaneously move the second elevator actuator to a second crashstop position along the axial 

 dimension based on a second default velocity command different from the first default velocity command. 1 1. The data storage device as recited in claim 10, wherein: the first elevator actuator comprises a first lead screw having a first pitch; the first default velocity command is based on a first nominal pitch representing the first pitch; the second elevator actuator comprises a second lead screw having a second pitch; and the second default velocity command is based on a second nominal pitch representing the second pitch. 12. The data storage device as recited in claim 1 1 , wherein after moving the first elevator actuator to the first crashstop position and moving the second elevator actuator to the second crashstop position, the control circuitry is further configured to: measure the first pitch of the first lead screw based on the first sensor signal; measure the second pitch of the second lead screw based on the second sensor signal; and simultaneously move the first and second elevator actuators based on the measured first pitch and the measured second pitch. 13. The data storage device as recited in claim 12, wherein the control circuitry is further configured to: measure the first pitch by turning the first lead screw a predetermined number of rotations and measuring a first displacement of the actuator arm along the axial dimension based on the first sensor signal; and 

 measure the second pitch by turning the second lead screw the predetermined number of rotations and measuring a second displacement of the at least part of the ramp along the axial dimension based on the second sensor signal. 14 The data storage device as recited in claim 13, wherein: the second sensor signal consists of a second sinusoidal sensor signal; and after moving the first elevator actuator to the first crashstop position and moving the second elevator actuator to the second crashstop position the control circuitry is further configured to: control the second elevator actuator to move the at least part of the ramp along the axial dimension away from the second crashstop position until a zero crossing is detected in the second sinusoidal sensor signal; and measure the first displacement and the second displacement after the zero crossing is detected in the second sinusoidal sensor signal. 15. A data storage device comprising: a first disk comprising a first disk surface; a second disk comprising a second disk surface; an actuator arm; a head coupled to a distal end of the actuator arm; a ramp for loading/unloading the head; a first elevator actuator configured to actuate the actuator arm along an axial dimension relative to the first and second disks; a second elevator actuator configured to actuate at least part of the ramp along the axial dimension; 

 a radial actuator configured to actuate the head radially over the first disk surface or the second disk surface; and a means for synchronizing a simultaneous movement of the first and second elevator actuators.
